reason: The issue is real: ChallengeStore.Issue inserts into byNonce, Consume deletes only when a nonce is presented, there is no expiry sweep, and handleChallenge does not call the IP limiter.
The issue is real: IPRateLimiter.Allow allocates one limiter per new IP in the limiters map and the implementation has no last-seen tracking, eviction, or size bound.

The issue is real: VerifyFiles validates only manifest-declared paths, while cmd_open passes all opened.Files to the adapter Install path, so extra safe adapter paths can be installed without a manifest hash entry.
The issue is real: cmd_send.go emits `echos friend add <name> echo:<their-echo-id>`, but echo IDs are bare 20-hex strings and friend add looks up and compares the argument literally.
The issue is real: tests found for identity creation call Ensure with an empty external key path or use `echos id` without `--key`; no test exercises createFromExternalKey or its error branches.
The issue is real: open tests invoke `echos open` without `--resume`, and no test reaches the c.Resume branch or runResume execution/error handling.

The issue is real as a test gap: app.ensureIdentity warns and continues on PublishKey failure, but there is no deterministic test pointing ECHOS_RELAY at a failing endpoint and asserting exit 0 plus the warning text.

The issue is real: existing envelope tests call VerifyFiles on matching round-trip inputs, while tamper tests fail at decrypt or signature verification; no test mutates Opened.Files or manifest size/hash to assert VerifyFiles rejection.

The issue is real: rolloutsByID's WalkDir callback returns nil for callback errors, including missing or unreadable entries, making the later err handling paths structurally unreachable for those errors.

The issue is real: SPEC.md documents magic `ECHS` and an obsolete manifest schema, while the implementation and approved contract use magic `ECHO` and the version/tool/session_id/project/title/sender_echo_id/sender_fingerprint/created_at/files[path,size,sha256] schema.

The issue is real: this is the same user-facing notation conflict as p_004; `echos id` and identity.EchoID use the bare 20-hex value, but the send failure hint tells users to pass an `echo:`-prefixed value that friend add will not match.
